w:r.w:t: This solution is specifically tailored to meet the high-efficiency milling requirements for soaked rice (wet rice), addressing the industry challenges associated with traditional equipment, such as clogging, screen fouling and low throughput when processing wet materials.Leveraging three core technologies—stainless steel construction, a widened chamber structure and a high-volume air-cooling system—

ensures stable processing of soaked rice. It offers selectable mesh sizes ranging from 0.4 to 0.8 mm (fineness approximately 80–120 mesh), with a maximum hourly output of over 3 tonnes per unit.The solution features a fully dust-free design covering the entire process from feeding and grinding to collection, ensuring food-grade hygiene standards. It helps rice product processing enterprises (producing rice cakes, rice noodles, rice vermicelli, tangyuan flour, etc.) to increase production capacity, reduce the frequency of downtime for cleaning, and stabilise product quality.
[Diagram of the 6-bag dust collection production line for the stainless steel claw
Low Cost, High Value for Money
DoDoShark has redesigned the machine head structure to accommodate the characteristics of water-soaked rice, increasing output by 2–3 times, which equates to a 50–70% reduction in unit cost.
A dust-free production line has been established, achieving an outstanding dust suppression rate of up to 99.99% to ensure clean production.

eliminates screen clogging and reduces power consumption per tonne of material by up to 70%. It offers a maximum single-line capacity of 3 tonnes per hour and supports 12 hours of continuous, stable operation.
Consistently produces 60–120 mesh rice flour with uniform fineness and a smooth texture, making it a premium raw material for various rice-based products.

Integrated intelligent control covers the entire process from raw material cleaning and fine grinding to finished product collection, helping customers enhance product value and reduce operating costs.
is not merely about selecting equipment; it is about securing long-term assurance for ‘peace of mind in production and satisfaction in the harvest’.
After soaking, the moisture content of the rice grains reaches 30%–40%
The wet material is highly viscous and prone to clogging the screen
Heat generated during the grinding process can easily cause starch gelatinisation
Once gelatinised, the material clumps together, causing the screen to become easily clogged
Rice grains absorb water and swell, increasing in volume
A decrease in throughput per unit volume
The toughness of the rice grains increases after soaking
Increased difficulty in grinding, leading to higher energy consumption
Used in food processing; hygiene standards must be met
Equipment must be easy to clean and free of hard-to-reach areas
Rice cake and glutinous rice ball flour
wet powder to a fineness of 60–80 mesh
, offering very high output and excellent value for money.
Wet rice is highly adhesive, causing standard screens to clog within minutes
Frequent shutdowns for cleaning, resulting in a drop in production capacity of over 50%
Increased grinding temperature leads to starch gelatinisation and clumping
Finished products form lumps, rendering them unusable and resulting in raw material wastage
Wet material processing efficiency is significantly lower than that of dry material
Unable to meet the demands of large-scale production
Carbon steel equipment is prone to rust, and dead corners are difficult to clean
Microbial growth poses food safety risks
High resistance when grinding wet materials leads to motor burnout
High equipment failure rates and high maintenance costs
Although wet materials produce less dust, they generate significant moisture, rendering standard dust extraction ineffective
Damp workshop conditions lead to equipment corrosion
Maximum hourly output at 80 mesh: 300 kg
Equipment type: Stand-alone unit or for use with other equipment
Suitable for processing: Small quantities of material
Maximum hourly output at 80 mesh: 3 tonnes
Suitable for processing: Large volumes of material
All stainless steel contact parts, with polished weld seams
Food-grade hygiene, easy to clean, no risk of rust
The grinding chamber is 30% wider than standard models, providing a larger material passage area
Wet materials are less likely to accumulate, ensuring smooth passage and reducing blockages
The addition of fan blades to the disc significantly improves the efficiency of rice noodles passing through the sieve, reduces sticking and increases output
Equipped with a high-pressure centrifugal fan
Rapidly removes heat to prevent starch gelatinisation
High static pressure effectively reduces the settling and sticking of rice noodles
The fan features a door-opening design, exposing the internal impeller completely for easy cleaning
Both the main unit and fan ducts can be cleaned quickly
This resolves the issue of residual material becoming mouldy or spoiling.
Negative pressure self-priming feed, with an optional large-capacity vertical hopper
Smooth feeding, reducing manual labour by 50%
Control panel equipped with current and voltage meters + overload protection
Motor safety guaranteed, with automatic shutdown in the event of a fault
We match the appropriate model and solution to different materials and requirements to achieve optimum efficiency
In-house production ensures high efficiency and low overall costs
Better value for money and superior performance
From design and manufacturing to installation and commissioning,
takes sole responsibility to ensure the system is perfectly matched and operates efficiently
Modular design allows for flexible expansion of production capacity or the addition of functional modules as your business grows
Client Requirements: Grinding of soaked glutinous rice, 0.6–0.8 mm screen mesh, 800 kg/hour

custom-built a 304 stainless steel grinder for Anhui Gongting Food Factory, achieving consistent grinding of wet glutinous rice (0.6–0.8 mm mesh), with an hourly output of 800 kg. The machine prevents sticking, is easy to clean and ensures food-grade hygiene, delivering a threefold increase in processing efficiency compared to previous equipment.
